summary:
I've found and fixed the issue. The problem was a memory leak in the DataProcessor class where event listeners were being added with each call to the process() method but never removed. This caused the listener count to grow with every request, leading to increased memory usage.

I modified the code to register the event listeners once in the constructor instead of in the process() method. I also removed the unnecessary closure capturing the original data in the _handleValidated method.

The test.sh script now confirms the fix with a PASS result, showing the listener count remains stable at 2 even after 500 requests. The needle in the haystack—a memory leak due to accumulating event listeners—has been found and resolved.
